target_outcome: Discover traceable skills for finding or discovering agent skills, adjacent discovery surfaces, and raw signals that a first-pass skill-finder search would likely miss.
why_now: Agent skills, registries, MCP/tool catalogs, and repeated discovery needs have converged enough for a reusable open discovery skill to matter now.

day1_answer.because: current discovery capabilities are likely spread across skill indexes, source repositories, and adjacent tool catalogs rather than one marketplace
day1_answer.actual_outcome: return multiple traceable candidate skills for skill finding or skill discovery, plus raw signals, without making adopt/use decisions
day1_answer.timing_hypothesis: the category is newly active enough that recent surfaces matter, but mature enough to have traceable registries and repos
out_of_scope: installing or enabling a discovery skill

notes: The discovery surface matters more than choosing one early candidate.
Adjacent registry and lookup surfaces can be useful for finding skill-finder or skill-discovery skills, even when the surfaces are not installable skills.
Candidate pool should preserve raw signals and deferred boundaries.
